titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Elizabeth Tyrrell, 26 July 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: William Upton Tyrrell expresses gratitude for a parcel and requests to be sent some Horlick's Malted Milk.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
26 July 1917. My dearest Mamy Thank you very much
indeed for the lovely parcel which came
yesterday and also your grand
letter. The parcel was A 1. the
cake was splendid and quite fresh.
Also the potted meat and toffee is
lovely. The toffee called triple
cream is particularly nice and I
am very fond of cafe au lait. Also that chicken you sent once
or twice was lovely and I think
might be acceptable in the future. I would also like to try just once some
Horlicks malted milk as people talk
a lot of it out here. Just send
it once or twice until I tell you
what it is like. I am now writing on the pad you
sent and it is just what I wanted.
It fits easily into my haversack. There are a lot of cyclists
round here but no trace of any
Canadian ones. The weather is very kind fine
again now for which thank
goodness. With very best love to all
from
your most affec Son
Willie
